title: paspalum vaginatum subsp. nanum
intro: Paspalum vaginatum subsp. nanum, also known as dwarf saltgrass, is a low-growing, perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
